pr.title: Match itoctarockstone and cleanup it_27CF to match it, rename to ityoshiegglay, rename it_2F2B to ityoshitongue
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched `itoctarockstone` by implementing `it_802E89D0` and marking the object Matching, then replaced provisional item filenames/headers with `ityoshiegglay` and `ityoshitongue`. The PR also dismantled the broad `it_27CF` umbrella header by moving prototypes into item-specific headers and updating fighter includes. There was no human review text in the slice; the decomp.dev bot reported the new Octarock stone match and old-unit broken-match entries for the renamed files.

key_files.role: Implemented `it_802E89D0`; added `ft/ftlib.h` and `baselib/random.h`; became a Matching object.
Changed `itoctarockstone.c` from NonMatching to Matching and updated object paths from `it_27CF.c`/`it_2F2B.c` to `ityoshiegglay.c`/`ityoshitongue.c`.
Rename target for `it_27CF.c`; includes `ityoshiegglay.h` and removes unrelated small wrapper functions from the old file.
New narrow header replacing `it_27CF.h`, keeping only Yoshi egg lay prototypes.
Deleted broad provisional header that had declared many unrelated item APIs.
Rename target for `it_2F2B.c`; include cleanup after switching to `ityoshitongue.h`.
Rename target for `it_2F2B.h` with identical header contents.
Received Fox blaster prototypes that had previously been provided through the broad `it_27CF` header.
Received Game & Watch manhole prototypes removed from local C declarations / old umbrella usage.
Changed selected `.sdata2` float/double labels from global `it_804D...` names to local `@NNN` labels.

what_changed: `configure.py` now builds `melee/it/items/itoctarockstone.c` as Matching instead of NonMatching.
`configure.py` now refers to `melee/it/items/ityoshitongue.c` instead of `it_2F2B.c`, and `melee/it/items/ityoshiegglay.c` instead of `it_27CF.c`; both new paths are listed as Matching.

`itoctarockstone.c` gained a real `it_802E89D0(Item_GObj* gobj, f32 horiz_speed, f32 min_vy, f32 max_vy)` implementation. It sets horizontal velocity from `horiz_speed * facing_dir`, queries a fighter via `ftLib_8008627C`, obtains its position via `ftLib_800866DC`, computes a vertical velocity using fall speed and distance, adds `HSD_Randf()` variation, clamps between `min_vy` and `max_vy`, and falls back to `0.0f` vertical velocity when no fighter gobj is found.
`it_27CF.c` was renamed to `ityoshiegglay.c`; the include changed from `it_27CF.h` to `ityoshiegglay.h`.

The old broad `it_27CF.h` and `it_27CF.dox` files were deleted. A new `ityoshiegglay.h` contains only the Yoshi egg lay-facing prototypes such as `it_802F2F34`, `it_27CF_UnkMotion*_Anim/Phys/Coll`, `it_27CF_Logic114_DmgReceived`, `it_27CF_Logic114_EvtUnk`, and `it_803F94A0`.
`it_2F2B.c`/`.h` were renamed to `ityoshitongue.c`/`.h`; the C file removed several now-unneeded includes while retaining Yoshi special-N dependencies.

Fighter files stopped including the catch-all `it/items/it_27CF.h` and switched to exact item headers where needed: examples include `itfoxblaster.h`, `itgamewatchmanhole.h`, `itgamewatchparachute.h`, `ityoshiegglay.h`, and `ityoshitongue.h`.
`itfoxblaster.h`, `itgamewatchmanhole.h`, and `itgamewatchparachute.h` gained public prototypes that had been declared through the removed broad header or locally in C files.

`it_26B1.c` replaced the `it_27CF.h` include with specific headers such as `itheart.h`, `itlinkbomb.h`, and `ittomato.h`, and performed a local variable cleanup from `bobOmbGObj` to `gobj` in `it_8026BE84` without evident logic changes.
`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` relabeled selected `.sdata2` constants at `0x804DD838`-`0x804DD848` and `0x804DDA18`-`0x804DDA28` from global `it_804D...` names to local `@NNN` labels.
smart_moves: The PR did not keep extending `it_27CF.h` as an umbrella for unrelated item APIs; it deleted it and pushed declarations into the owning item headers.

File names were upgraded to item-role names (`ityoshiegglay`, `ityoshitongue`) while unresolved internal function names like `it_27CF_*` and address-based `it_802F*` symbols were left intact, avoiding unsupported over-naming.
The Octarock stone implementation preserves explicit intermediate math, randomization, and clamp order, and decomp.dev confirmed `it_802E89D0` reached 100% for 268 bytes.
Moving consumers to exact headers made dependency ownership clearer across fighter code and reduced accidental inclusion of unrelated item declarations.
Relabeling literal constants as local `@NNN` symbols corrected symbol scope for matching/data hygiene instead of preserving fake global `it_804D...` names.
decomp_lessons: Once a provisional item file is identified, prefer renaming the file/header and updating `configure.py` in the same PR so the build graph, object matching status, and include paths stay synchronized.
Broad unknown headers can hide ownership mistakes. Replace them with narrow headers per item, then update fighter sources to include the exact item they call.
Do not rename internal symbols beyond evidence: this PR renamed files to Yoshi item names but kept address/old-prefix function names where the true semantic names were not established.
Compiler-local `.sdata2` constants should be represented as local labels when they are not truly global exports.
Bot match reports may show old-unit regressions after file renames; compare against `configure.py` and the new unit names before treating every old-path entry as a codegen regression.
naming_conventions: Item filenames/headers use lowercase `it` + item name, e.g. `itfoxblaster.h`, `itgamewatchmanhole.h`, `ityoshiegglay.h`, `ityoshitongue.h`.
Old address-based and provisional function names can remain inside newly named files until better symbol evidence exists, e.g. `it_27CF_UnkMotion0_Anim` inside `ityoshiegglay.h`.
Local float/double constants in `symbols.txt` may use `@NNN` labels instead of invented global `it_804D...` names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `it_802E89D0` uses a small inline `getX` helper for absolute X distance instead of a library absolute-value call; the bot confirmed the function matched after this implementation.

The vertical-velocity expression keeps visible temporaries such as `float new_var` and `x = (new_var = fall_speed * (speed * speed)); x *= 0.5f;`, which is likely matching-sensitive even though no review comment explains it.
The function preserves call/order dependencies: `ftLib_8008627C`, `ftLib_800866DC`, `HSD_Randf()`, then high/low clamps on `ip->x40_vel.y`.
The diff keeps an existing Kirby comment/pattern, `FAKE MATCH: comma operator required for regalloc`, in `ftKb_SpecialAirHi3_Phys`; future cleanup should not simplify that expression casually.
Updating `.sdata2` symbol labels from global names to local `@NNN` labels is a data-matching tactic used alongside the new `itoctarockstone` match.
review_feedback: No human PR body, review comments, or reviews were captured in this slice.
decomp.dev bot reported 2 new matches for GALE01: `main/melee/it/items/itoctarockstone` `it_802E89D0` (+268 bytes, 0% -> 100%) and `.sdata2` (+5 bytes, 80% -> 100%).

The same bot report listed 21 broken matches under the old `it_27CF` and `it_2F2B` unit paths, plus one old `it_27CF` `.sdata2` regression; the slice has no human explanation, and the diff shows those units were renamed.
follow_up_queries: Search for any remaining includes of `it/items/it_27CF.h` or `it/items/it_2F2B.h` after the rename.
Audit `ityoshiegglay.h` for remaining `it_27CF_*` names and rename only when symbol/semantic evidence is stronger.
Check whether decomp.dev old-unit broken-match entries for `it_27CF` and `it_2F2B` are purely rename accounting by comparing the new `ityoshiegglay` and `ityoshitongue` objects.
Search for old global symbols `it_804DD838`, `it_804DD83C`, `it_804DD840`, `it_804DD848`, `it_804DDA18`, `it_804DDA1C`, `it_804DDA20`, and `it_804DDA28` before reintroducing them.
Consider giving `it_27CF_DatAttrs` a Yoshi egg lay-specific type name only after confirming all users and data layout.
